propositions: Add a CI job that extracts the README quickstart's fenced CLI command block and executes those exact lines end-to-end (init, create, ready, claim, close) in an isolated throwaway git repo.
The job must fail the build if any extracted README command errors or changes exit code, catching wrong printed commands like a missing 'append' verb or a stale hard-coded id.
The gate runs against an installed rebar (clean pipx/pip install) so it also catches packaging and entry-point regressions.
A static help-name check or the independent probe alone is insufficient because it cannot catch wrong subcommands/arguments, stale ids, or ordering/state errors printed in the README.
scripts/probe-rebar.sh is retained as a complementary broader golden-path smoke and must remain referenced by CI so it does not silently rot.
This gate depends on prior fixes to the session-log command and the repaired runnable quickstart block, and must land after them.
